characters.name: Annie Porter
characters.description: Annie PorterSpeciesHumanRelativesTom Porter(father)Natasha Porter(mother, deceased)Kevin Porter(older brother)Played byJenny DruganFirst AppearanceTasha (episode)Last EpisodeMisery Loves Company Anamarie "Annie" Porteris a fictional character in the 1990 reboot of theLand of the Lostfranchise. She was played by actressJenny Drugan. Annie is the 11-year-old daughter ofTom Porter. She is very bright and intuitive for her age and resents her family (especially Kevin whom she considers a "dork") for treating her like a baby. She has had to grow up rather quickly in a hostile environment and has shown that she is more than capable of taking care of herself on many occasions. Annie is a vegetarian, and one of her responsibilities is maintaining her garden at the base of the treehouse. Annie is the 11-year-old daughter ofTom Porter. characters.name: Balen
characters.description: SirBalenwas squire who pretended to be a knight from King Arthur's Court. After "borrowing" his master's armor and sword while he's asleep, Balen set out into the moors and was enshrouded in a "blue bubbling fog" and ended up in theLand of the Lostjust in time to saveChristafrom a gang ofSleestaks. AfterShungheard of this, he sentKeegandNimafter Balen's sword, thinking it to be a "magic blade". Balen's desire to protect "M'lady Christa" caused friction with a jealousKevin Porteras he set out to escort her home not aware that she could take care of herself. Kevin went after him, they again encounterScarface(whom Balen referred to as "The king of all dragons"). After eluding him, the ground shook and another doorway appeared. Balen offered Kevin his morning star in recognition of his bravery and disappeared through the gate.

characters.name: Beauregard Jackson
characters.description: Beauregard JacksonOccupationAstronaut/PilotSpeciesHumanRelativesNoneAccomplishmentsescaped theLand of the LostPlayed byRon MasakFirst AppearanceHurricaneLast EpisodeHurricaneBeauregard Jacksonis a minor character in theLand of the Lost. He was played by actorRon Masak. Jackson is a native of Forth Worth, Texas in an alternate future version of the 1990's, or at least twenty years into the future from when theMarshall's became trapped in theLand. Jackson is a hypersonic glider pilot who becomes briefly trapped in the Land when Will, tinkering with thecrystalmatrix Table in aMountain Pylon, causes a time doorway to open high above the ground. Jackson's glider was re-entering Earth's atmosphere over Ecuador while returning from a routine transit between Phoenix Port and Space Station 5. His glider's tail was suddenly severed in his fall, but he fell to earth and survived when his parachute got snared in a tree by his parachute.

characters.name: Big Alice
characters.description: Big Alice20091974OccupationProtector and motherSpeciesAllosaurusRelativesJunior(son)AccomplishmentsDriven off theMarshall Familyseveral times; foughtGrumpyto a standstillPlayed byStop Motion (1974)CGI (2009)First AppearanceThe Sleestak GodLast EpisodeScarab CGI (2009) Big Alice(AKA Alice) is one the theDinosaursof theLand of the Lost. She was created using stop-motion animation, she was also created with CGI. Alice is an Allosaurus, a huge apex predator of the carnosaur order ofdinosaurs. While allosaurs were typically smaller than tyrannosaurs, Alice rivalsGrumpy(a Tyrannosaurus rex) in sheer size, almost forty feet long, twenty feet tall, and approximately fifteen short tons in weight. Like all allosaurs, she is bipedal, dragging her tail behind her, with short, but very strong front limbs. Although extremely aggressive and territorial, Alice also displays a maternal side, protecting her offspring and nest within theLost City. She is often seen trying to chase off theMarshallsand "arguing" with Grumpy, who dominates the territory on the other side of the crevasse. While she strongly resembles Grumpy, in size, form and temperament, Alice is distinguishable by her green coloration, in contrast with Grumpy's mottled brown.

characters.name: Big Guy
characters.description: TheBig Guywas a large, long-neckedApatosauruswho lived in theLand of the Lost. Big Guy had a long and small head and a whip-like tail. He frequently appeared around the Porters, mostly browsing on the vegetation, either at big lakes or at the Porters' treehouse. He once helpedAnnie Porterdown from a tree whenKevin Portertook away a ladder and savedTashafrom theSleestaks. Onone occasion, he is seen with another Apatosaurus, possibly his mate.

characters.name: Botts Pol
characters.description: Botts Polwas a character in the Land of the Lost series. He was similar to aSleestak, physically but he had the brains and ability to think like no other. What is unknown about Botts is that he might not even be a sleestak.

characters.name: Captain Elmo Diggs
characters.description:
CaptainElmo Diggswas a U.S. cavalry officer man who once encountered the strandedMarshalls.
The Marshalls meet Diggs, claimed the IndianLone Wolf, who was desperate to get medicine to his sick people, had stolen the medicine that his men need if they hope to survive. At first, Diggs and Lone Wolf and were suspicious of each other, butJack Marshalleventually brokered a peace between them. Eventually Diggs and the Indian medicine man rode off together, on one horse, hoping to return home to their time.

characters.name: Captain Ruben Van de Meer
characters.description: Captain Ruben Van de MeerJack and William BlandingsOccupationCaptain of theFlying DutchmanSpeciesHuman (possible Ghost)RelativesNoneAccomplishmentsescaped theLand of the LostPlayed byRex HolmanFirst AppearanceFlying DutchmanLast EpisodeFlying DutchmanCaptain Ruben Van de Meeris a minor character in theLand of the Lost. He was played by actorRex Holman. Captain Ruben Van de Meer is a man of about 40 years of age, ruddy complexion and reddish-blond hair, and a full beard and mustache wearing 17th Century naval uniform. Sentenced to serve solitary duty forever on the Flying Dutchman for unspecified "dereliction of duty," he is quite lonely. He is also rather scheming and an able manipulator. Captain Ruben Van de Meer was the Captain of theFlying DutchmantheMarshallencountered within theMist Marsh. He is the sole crew member of the Flying Dutchman, attended only by the ghosts of other crew members. (These voices are not merely hallucinations since the Marshalls can hear them, too.) characters.name: Cha-Ka
characters.description: Cha-KaOccupationInapplicableSpeciesPakuniRelativesTa(brother),Sa(sister)AccomplishmentsrescuedDopeyfrom the tar, stole an egg shell fromBig Alice, passed the test ofThe BuilderPlayed byPhilip PaleyFirst AppearanceCha-Ka (episode)Last EpisodeMedicine ManCha-Kais a fictional character in "Land of the Lost." He started out as a recurring character and became a main character in theThird Season. He was played by child actorPhilip Paley. Cha-Ka is a small adolescent hominid resembling the extinct Paranthropus apes of Earth. He is covered in hair all over his body except on his face, palms and feet. He is loyal and friendly, more eager to learn than his siblings,Ta and Sa, thereby becoming more intelligent. Cha-Ka is one of thePakuni, the indigenous hominid species in theLand of the Lost. He is the most favorably disposed of the Pakuni toward theMarshallssince they rescued him fromGrumpyand splinted his broken leg. He eventually forms a close friendship with the family, particularly withHolly Marshall, helping them whenever possible. He occasionally visits the Marshalls in their cave inHigh Bluff. characters.name: Christa
characters.description: Christais a young woman from Earth, who was trapped in theLand of the Lostthrough similar circumstances tothe Porters, sometime during the 1960s. Not much is known about her life, but it is revealed she was born in Chicago and raised in San Francisco. At one point after her arrival, her family built a raft to try to sail to the other side of the ocean within the land of the Lost, hoping to find signs of civilization. In a subsequent storm, Christa was washed overboard, but she found refuge clinging to a floating tree trunk where after several days of drifting she found her way to a beach. Separated from her parents, Christa grew up practically alone, and by the time the Porters encountered her, she had repressed almost everything about her previous life. Christa is fiercely independent and feels safe and at home in the jungle. However, she's not oblivious to its many dangers and uses her knowledge of its topography, inhabitants, and various medicinal plants to aid the Porters whenever she can. She is fluent inPakunioften greeting her human friends with "Acuba Ne" to express both "hello" and "goodbye".

characters.name: Colonel Roscoe T. Post
characters.description: Colonel Roscoe T. PostOccupationAdventurerSpeciesHumanRelativesNone knownAccomplishmentslanded in theLand of the LostPlayed byDavid HealyFirst AppearanceHot Air ArtistLast EpisodeHot Air ArtistColonel Roscoe T. Postwas a minor character in theLand of the Lost. He was played by actorDavid Healy. Colonel Roscoe T. Post a slightly overweight man probably in his forties with black hair and a handlebar moustache. Although able to present himself as well-mannered and charming, the Colonel (as everyone calls him) seems to have an underdeveloped sense of empathy, concerned more for his own celebrity and lucrative opportunities than the well-being or feelings of others. Colonel Roscoe T. Post a slightly overweight man probably in his forties with black hair and a handlebar moustache. Although able to present himself as well-mannered and charming, the Colonel (as everyone calls him) seems to have an underdeveloped sense of empathy, concerned more for his own celebrity and lucrative opportunities than the well-being or feelings of others. Always on the lookout for opportunities to advance his celebrity and affluence, he is not very careful about revealing too much about his schemes. He openly shares his plans to exploit Cha-Ka in front of a disgusted Holly, who immediately informs Jack. Quite possibly, given his disregard for others, he saw nothing wrong with exploiting Cha-Ka for wealth and celebrity. He might have even been expecting the Marshalls to participate. He appears unwilling or unable to regard Cha-Ka as an intelligent being with feelings. When he hears Cha-Ka speak for the first time, he refers to Cha-Ka as "it," and "the missing link," seeing only money-making possibilities by putting Cha-Ka on public display for paying onlookers. Without any regard for Cha-Ka's own wishes, the Colonel attempts to kidnap him, reneging on his promise to take the Marshalls with him when he leaves. When thwarted in his plans and must depart without Cha-Ka, he seems to take the loss in stride, even bidding the Marshalls a fond farewell, asking that they remember him.

characters.name: Corny
characters.description: CornyOccupationPet toHolly MarshallSpeciesUnicornRelativesNoneAccomplishmentsNonePlayed byUnrevealedFirst AppearanceAbominable SnowmanLast EpisodeAbominable SnowmanCornyis one of the indigenous animals known as a toki of theLand of the Lost. Corny is a miniature brown horse with a single uncurved spiral horn extending from his forehead. He stands at approximately 10 hands in height ("hands" being the unit of measure for the height of horses, with one hand being equal to four inches and a horse's height being taken at the shoulder). His horn is approximately 9 inches long. From forehead to rump, Corny is approximately four feet long. Despite being a wild animal used as a food source for the Tappa, Corny is extremely docile. He remains calm when approached by Jack and Will, despite never having seen them before. And does not resist when mounted by Cha-Ka. He stands at approximately 10 hands in height ("hands" being the unit of measure for the height of horses, with one hand being equal to four inches and a horse's height being taken at the shoulder). His horn is approximately 9 inches long. From forehead to rump, Corny is approximately four feet long. Despite being a wild animal used as a food source for the Tappa, Corny is extremely docile. characters.name: Emily
characters.description: EmilyOccupationMother and protector ofDopeySpeciesDinosaurRelativesDopey(possible adopted son)Accomplishmentspulled Dopey from theTar PitsPlayed bystop motionFirst AppearanceCha-Ka (episode)Last EpisodeCorneredEmilyis one of thedinosaursin theLand of the Lost. She was created using stop-motion and chroma-key special effects. Emily is a gray-skinned Brontosaurus. Like her comparative versions of Earth's Jurassic period, Emily was a herbivore, approximately 72 feet in length and 17 tons in weight. Despite her immense size, Emily was normally quite gentle; she not typically a threat to theMarshallsand showed tender, maternal feelings toward her adopted son Dopey. Though under extraordinary circumstances, she can become quite aggressive.On one occasion, during a drought, she charged Will and Holly Marshall, who were forced to seek refuge in a pylon until Emily gave up. characters.name: Enik
characters.description: EnikOccupationScientist/ResearcherSpeciesAltrusianRelativesNone knownAccomplishmentshas studied crystal technology and fifth dimensional physics, saved the lives of many of theMarshall FamilyPlayed byWalker EdmistonFirst AppearanceThe StrangerLast EpisodeScarab"That is illogical..." - Enik Enikis a major recurring character in theLand of the Lost. He was played byWalker Edmiston. Enik resembles a Sleestak, but he is more orange/brown in color with a broader mouth, and doesn't make the hissing breathing sound that is associated with the Sleestak. He does have claws, but they are small and rather blunt in comparison to those of the Sleestak, and Enik's smallest claw is positioned on his hand adjacent to the other two, similar to the disposition of fingers on a human hand, unlike that of the regular Sleestaks, whose smallest claw is vestigial and projects from what would be the wrist on a human being. He is also significantly shorter than the average Sleestak (about the height of an average human male). Unlike the other Sleestak, he is not at all bothered by light and can even venture into daylight (which might suggest that the Sleestak had devolved a bit after living underground since the collapse of their civilization). Because of the height difference, the Sleestak often refer to him as "The Dwarf," but being more enlightened than they are, this type of disparagement doesn't bother him. Despite this, Sleestak do hold him some regard as far as leadership and guidance are involved, but they still have a tense relationship and do not always respect his advice. characters.name: Fred
characters.description: FredOccupationServant ofThe ZarnSpeciesRobotRelativesInapplicableAccomplishmentsdrove off Rick and Will Marshall from theZarn's ShipPlayed bystop motionFirst AppearanceGravity StormLast EpisodeGravity StormFredwas a minor character onLand of the Lost. It was created using stop-animation. Fred is a bipedal, armless, mechanical dinosaur-like construct of an unspecified type of metal, around ten feet in height. The head is bird-like, with two luminous eyes, and a beak. It also emits a mechanical squawking sound. Fred was a mechanical dinosaur-like creation of theZarn's, sent after Will and Rick to drive them away from his ship. It was a relentless pursuer, a faultless tracker and unaffected by the sudden surge of gravity that was capable of incapacitating theMarshalls, and evenSpike. While not quite as fast as the Marshalls, it was inexhaustible, able to maintain a constant pace without tiring. It was also able to easily scale the cliffs that Rick and Will Marshall struggled to climb, despite his lack of forelimbs. He was destroyed by a bolt of lightning on top of a ledge. characters.name: Grumpy
characters.description: GrumpyOccupationPredatorSpeciesTyrannosaurus rexRelativesNone knownAccomplishmentshas consistently chased and annoyed theMarshallsand thePakuniPlayed bystop motionFirst AppearanceCha-Ka (episode)Last EpisodeMedicine Man Grumpywas a native to theLand of the Lostin the 1974 to 1976 version ofLand of the Lost. Grumpy is possibly the top apex predator in the Land of the Lost but forBig Aliceand possiblyLulu. Standing 18-20 feet tall and fifty feet long, he is bipedal and gray in color, often dragging his tail. He is constantly pursuing the Marshalls or roaring at them at the cave entrance to their home in High Bluff. In the latter occasions, the Marshalls usually repel him with alarge log sharpened at the tipthat they force down his throat. While this tactic is effective in causing Grumpy to retreat, Grumpy eventually manages to dislodge the log. Stubborn and territorial, he also feuds a lot withBig Aliceat the crevasse separating their hunting grounds. His main food source seem to be theCoelophysiswhich roam the area. characters.name: Grumpy (2009)
characters.description: GrumpyOccupationPredatorSpeciesTyrannosaurus RexRelativesNone knownAccomplishmentsfrequently harassedDr. Rick Marshalland his expedition, as well as adding them in foilingEnik's planPlayed byCGIFirst AppearanceLand of the LostGrumpyis a large, extremely aggressive male Tyrannosaurus rex featured in the 2009 adaptation of theLand of the Lostfranchise. He serves as the secondary antagonist of the film, but later turned anti-villain, and became a hero during the fight against the Sleestaks. Standing 18-20 feet tall and measuring 40 feet long, Grumpy is a large Tyrannosaurus rex with a primarily grayish-black skin tone with black tiger-like stripes and forehead featuring three rows of prominent, ridge like scales with the middle row running across his back and ending near the tip of his tail. Grumpy features a small scar on the right side of his upper lip, consisting of 3 parallel slash marks. Grumpy is apparently a reptile of some age and incredibly intelligent for a dinosaur, with the ability to understand the meaning of an insult fromDr. Rick Marshall, upon which he developed a grudge against the paleontologist. This perceived slight serves as Grumpy's motivation throughout the vast majority of his former antagonism, compelling him to stalk Rick and his expedition team across the entire Land of the Lost. Grumpy is shown to have an extreme aggressive disposition, which Dr. Marshall attributes to as some type of intestinal blockage. However, Dr. Marshall insulting his brain size seems to be another factor in his aggressive tendencies.

He serves as the secondary antagonist of the film, but later turned anti-villain, and became a hero during the fight against the Sleestaks. Standing 18-20 feet tall and measuring 40 feet long, Grumpy is a large Tyrannosaurus rex with a primarily grayish-black skin tone with black tiger-like stripes and forehead featuring three rows of prominent, ridge like scales with the middle row running across his back and ending near the tip of his tail. Grumpy features a small scar on the right side of his upper lip, consisting of 3 parallel slash marks. Grumpy is apparently a reptile of some age and incredibly intelligent for a dinosaur, with the ability to understand the meaning of an insult fromDr. Rick Marshall, upon which he developed a grudge against the paleontologist. This perceived slight serves as Grumpy's motivation throughout the vast majority of his former antagonism, compelling him to stalk Rick and his expedition team across the entire Land of the Lost. Grumpy is shown to have an extreme aggressive disposition, which Dr. Marshall attributes to as some type of intestinal blockage. However, Dr. Marshall insulting his brain size seems to be another factor in his aggressive tendencies.

characters.name: Holly Marshall
characters.description: Holly MarshallOccupationStudentSpeciesHumanRelativesRick Marshall(father),Mrs. Marshall(mother, name unrevealed, deceased),Will Marshall(brother),Jack Marshall(uncle)AccomplishmentsSaved father and brother several times from theSleestak, named most of the dinosaurs in the Land of the LostPlayed byKathy ColemanFirst AppearanceCha-Ka (episode)Last EpisodeMedicine Man"Oh, Fat Rats!" - Holly Marshall Holly Marshallis one of the main characters of theLand of the Lost. She is the daughter ofRick Marshall, the sister ofWill Marshalland the niece ofJack Marshall. She is the youngest human living in theLand of the Lost. She was played by actressKathy Coleman. Holly is a smart, creative and energetic young girl, having named much of the life and activity in the Land of the Lost. She can also be very resourceful when she needs it. She is often working hard doing chores for her father even if her brother Will is often leading her off on adventures, during which she is often trying to be a voice of reason. Due to her age, she is usually frightened at most problems, and she has mild acrophobia (fear of heights). She and her brother fight sometimes, even over trivial matters as he is often teasing or mocking her, but when the problem gets tough, they are able to work together. She is often seen wearing a red-checkered shirt with brown pants. She sometimes wears her blonde hair in a pair of braids. characters.name: Jack Marshall
characters.description: Jack MarshallOccupationPark RangerSpeciesHumanRelativesRick Marshall(brother),Will Marshall(nephew),Holly Marshall(neice)Accomplishmentsdefended his brother's children from theSleestakPlayed byRon HarperFirst AppearanceAfter-ShockLast EpisodeMedicine ManJohn "Jack" Marshallis a main character in theLand of the Lost, replacing the character ofRick Marshallplayed bySpencer Milligan. He was played byRon Harper Jack Marshall is a tall, athletic male with brown hair and eyes. Dressed in a similar ranger uniform to his brother, he was a logical and rational person capable of using deception to further his goals. He wasn't above using violence against the Sleestak, such as homemade bombs and flare guns, but presumably out of consideration forEnik, he avoided deadly force when possible, using such tactics to scare instead of deliberately taking Sleestak lives. Not much is known about Jack Marshall's past prior to his arrival in theLand of the Lost. It is known he is likely an American park ranger like his brother,Rick Marshall, possibly in the American Southwest (possibly near the Grand Canyon). He is also the uncle ofWill MarshallandHolly Marshall. When Rick, Will, and Holly go missing on a river expedition, he began searching for them after six months by taking the same route they had taken, and eventually falling through the same access point between Earth and theLand of the Lostwhich had claimed Rick and the kids. characters.name: Junior
characters.description: JuniorOccupationStudentSpeciesDinosaurRelativesBig Alice(mother)Accomplishmentsleft his shell toCha-Ka, unintentionally routedTa and SaPlayed byStop-Motion AnimationFirst AppearanceThe TestLast EpisodeThe MusicianJunioris one of the dinosaurs of theLand of the Lost. He was created using stop-motion animation. Junior was a juvenile Allosaurus around three feet tall and four feet long with grey skin, short fore limbs and a long tail. He couldn't roar and instead made a beeping noise. He was completely docile and completely harmless to theMarshalls. Junior was the name of a young Allosaurus that lived in theLand of the Lost. characters.name: Keeg
characters.description: Keegis one ofShung's underlings. Keeg appears to be taller thanNim. Keeg appears to be the dumb one as he did not know what a chariot was. However, he is a strong fighter (his peripheral vision is his chief weakness) and an excellent tracker. Keeg is the more submissive of the pair. Despite their differences, both he and Nim try to please Shung with whatever they catch for him.

characters.name: Keela
characters.description: Keelais a sorceress from another time who was banished to theLand of the Lostfor not using her magic to aid Magas. She arrived through a greenDimensional gatewaylocated in a desert on the other side of the Land of the Lost. Keela is gifted in the ways of magic and sorcery but is not powerful enough to send thePorters(or herself) back home. The Porters eventually helped Keela defeat her enemy,Magas, whom she had turned into a one-eyed beast. Keela later acceptedAnnie Porteras an informal apprentice.
